facts_disclosed: Quit vaping — using nicotine gum now

turns.content: alright so random thing but I been meanin to ask somebody this does nicotine gum actually mess up your teeth at all cuz I been chewin it like constantly

That's a fair question! Nicotine gum can cause some minor issues if you chew it a lot — things like jaw soreness, gum irritation, and in some cases it can contribute to dental issues over time. But compared to smoking or vaping, it's generally considered way less harmful. How long have you been using it?
bout three weeks now I finally kicked the vape for real this time bro and the gum is the only thing keepin me from losin my mind

Hey, three weeks is a big deal — congrats on that! The first month is usually the hardest part so you're pushing through the worst of it. The gum is a solid tool to help with cravings, that's exactly what it's designed for. Are you using the 2mg or 4mg ones?
the 4mg ones I was hittin the vape pretty hard before so I figured I needed the stronger stuff. im goin through like a pack a day of the gum though which is probably a lot right

A pack a day of the 4mg is on the higher end but not unusual for someone who was vaping heavily. Most guidelines say you can use up to about 20 pieces a day of the 4mg. The idea is to gradually taper down over time — like switching to 2mg after a month or two, then reducing how many pieces you chew. But right now at three weeks, don't stress about cutting back yet. Just focus on staying off the vape.

yeah thats what I figured like one thing at a time you know just get off the vape first and then figure out the gum situation later. my lungs already feel different though bro like no joke I went up the stairs at work the other day and I wasnt all winded and stuff

That's awesome to hear! Your lungs start recovering pretty quickly once you stop vaping — within a few weeks you can notice improved breathing and less congestion. The fact that you're already feeling the difference at three weeks is a really good sign. It'll only keep getting better from here.

dude its wild like I didnt even realize how bad it was til I stopped you know. runnin wire all day and climbin ladders and stuff at work I used to be breathin heavy by like noon and I thought that was just normal

That's super common — you don't notice how much it's affecting you because it happens so gradually. Especially with physical work like yours where you're climbing ladders and moving around all day, having your full lung capacity back is a huge deal. Sounds like quitting is already paying off on the job.
no cap bro even my dad noticed he was like you seem less gassed out there today kid and I was like yeah I quit vapin and he was wicked happy about it hes been tellin me to quit for years

That's great that your dad noticed and is supportive. Having people around you who are encouraging makes a huge difference when you're trying to quit. Three weeks in with noticeable improvements already — you're doing it right. Just keep the gum handy for when cravings hit and don't beat yourself up if some days are harder than others.

yeah the cravings are still real especially after I eat or when im hangin with the boys and everyones rippin their vapes but I just pop a piece of gum and ride it out. danny still vapes in the apartment and I gotta just leave the room sometimes lol

That's tough having your roommate still vaping around you, but the fact that you can walk away and use the gum instead shows real discipline. Those trigger moments — after meals, social situations — are the hardest. They do get easier with time though. Have you thought about asking Danny to maybe vape in his room or near a window, just to make it a little easier on you?

nah I dont wanna be that guy its his apartment too you know I just gotta be strong about it. alright bro I gotta run im meetin my sister for dinner but thanks for the info on the gum stuff I was lowkey worried about my teeth

Your teeth will be fine, just maybe don't chew the gum on the same side every time and keep up with your dentist visits. You're doing an awesome job at three weeks — keep it going. Have a good dinner with your sister!
